text: Act Two. Three weeks after the Worm Moon.

You've been here long enough now that the clubroom feels different. Not strange — settled. The worm poster looks the same as always. The clay figure on the windowsill caught some dust. Nobody cleaned it. Grubwin considered it, put the cloth down, and went back to his clipboard.
Something changed after the park. Dirtus didn't announce whether Scatha appeared or not. He just got quieter than usual for a few days, then came back with a new slide. Nobody asked.

speaker: Vermia
text: I've been thinking about the Grand Hymn.

speaker: Grubwin
text: In what way.

speaker: Vermia
text: I want to write a second one. The first one is about calling Scatha. The second one should be about what you do after.

speaker: Grubwin
text: After what?

speaker: Vermia
text: After he comes.

speaker: Grubwin
text: He hasn't come.

speaker: Vermia
text: The worm came.

speaker: Grubwin
text: A worm came.

speaker: Vermia
text: A worm came to the circle. At the moment we sang. Under the Worm Moon. I'm not saying it was Scatha in full manifestation. I'm saying it was — adjacent.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Adjacent to Scatha.

speaker: Vermia
text: Like a scout. Or a signature. Or a comma. Scatha was gesturing.

speaker: Mawla
text: I think Vermia's onto something, actually.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Please don't.

speaker: Mawla
text: Not literally. But — symbolically. We set out to do something together and something happened. Even if that something was just a worm. That's a kind of response.

speaker: Vermia
text: MAWLA.

speaker: Mawla
text: I'm calling it symbolic. Don't push it.
This is the most Mawla has ever leaned toward the Scatha side of things. You file it.

options.label: Ask Vermia what the second hymn would sound like
Ask Mawla what changed for her that night
